headline: Oklahoma's ground game overwhelms Michigan in wire-to-wire rout
body: Oklahoma's rushing attack proved too much for Michigan's defense on Saturday, as the Sooners controlled the line of scrimmage from kickoff to final whistle. Sam Franklin's 204 combined rushing yards set the tone early, and Oklahoma never relinquished momentum. While Michigan's passing game found moments of rhythm through Gabriel's 290 yards and Charleston's five receptions, the Wolverines couldn't generate enough explosive plays to keep pace with a Sooners offense that dictated tempo and field position throughout the afternoon.

The decisive schism came in the second quarter when Oklahoma's rushing attack began to overwhelm Michigan's interior. Bernard punched in a touchdown on the ground, and Walsh added a receiving score that extended Oklahoma's lead to a margin the Wolverines could not bridge. Gabriel moved the ball competently in stretches, but Michigan's offense stalled whenever it needed a critical third down, while Oklahoma's ground-first approach methodically drained clock and forced Michigan into reactive mode. A late fumble by Michigan summed up the afternoon: the Wolverines were chasing rather than leading.

Oklahoma's defense, anchored by Jones's nine tackles, bent but rarely broke under Michigan's air assault. The Sooners allowed 283 passing yards yet surrendered only a single touchdown, a testament to scheme and pressure timing. For all of Gabriel's completion percentage and yardage, Michigan's red zone efficiency evaporated when it mattered most. Oklahoma's staff engineered a game plan that exploited Michigan's defensive gaps in run support and executed with the kind of discipline that wins games on the road.
turningPoint: Bernard's rushing touchdown in the second quarter expanded Oklahoma's lead and shifted momentum irreversibly, as Michigan's defense lost structural integrity against the ground attack.

unsungHero.name: Jones
unsungHero.why: Nine tackles and constant presence in the backfield made Franklin's rushing lanes possible and kept Michigan's passing game under siege; his work doesn't appear in the stat line but shaped field position all afternoon
